response.justification: Sarah Smith brings 16+ years of software engineering experience and a recent foray into AI-agent infrastructure, including MCP server development and agentic workflow tooling that directly maps to listed key technologies. However, the role carries a hard requirement of 8+ years delivering production-grade AI solutions — a bar she falls dramatically short of, with AI-specific work appearing only from April 2025 onward. Python, the dominant language in the AI/ML ecosystem and explicitly required, is entirely absent from her skill profile. There is likewise no evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or customer-facing technical consulting, all of which are core to this staff-level, forward-deployed role.
strengths: Direct hands-on experience with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and agentic workflow infrastructure, matching a listed key technology verbatim
Proven staff-level engineering track record with mentoring, community workshops, and cross-stack technical ownership across multiple companies
Production-scale TypeScript/Node.js delivery — high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby, AWS CDK-based pricing systems at Optilyz — demonstrating cloud and distributed-systems credibility
concerns: AI/ML experience appears to be under one year; the role mandates 8+ years of production-grade AI solution delivery, making this the most disqualifying gap
Python is absent from the resume entirely despite being one of two explicitly required primary languages for this position

No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation or observability frameworks, multi-agent systems (LangGraph, CrewAI), or customer-facing pre-sales and technical discovery engagements
